data.title: Community Trust Bancorp declares $0.47 dividend
data.description:
• Community Trust Bancorp (NASDAQ:CTBI) declares $0.47/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.63%
  • Payable Jan. 2; for shareholders of record Dec. 15; ex-div Dec. 12.
  • See CTBI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Hotel stocks weighed down by Hilton's lowered outlook
data.description:
Hilton Worldwide Holdings (NYSE:HLT) lowered its outlook for the full year and expects to receive less income for each room as soft macroeconomic trends in the U.S. and China drives down demand for the hotel chain’s modestly-priced properties. Pressure on Hilton (HLT) shares, however, was mitigated by better-than-expected profits and a modest increase in system-wide revenue.

For the year, Hilton (HLT) lowered its topline revenue per available room, or RevPAR outlook, to increase by 2.0% to 2.5% from earlier guidance of up 2.0% to 3.0%, and lowered its outlook for net income to be between $1.405B to $1.429B from the initial forecast of $1.532B to $1.555B. Adjusted EPS outlook remained unchanged at $6.93 to $7.03, but was below the consensus estimate of $7.06.

Estimates for Q4 showed even further deterioration in RevPar, expected to increase by just 1.0% to 2.0% with adjusted EPS of $1.57 to $1.67 (versus $1.92 in Q3), below the consensus estimate of $1.76.

For the currently reported quarter, RevPar was up 1.4%, down from +3.5% in the previous quarter and down from +6.8% in the same quarter last year, underscoring the system-wide decline in occupancy in the U.S. and China. In Q3 RevPar in China was down 3.4% versus a year ago and increased by just 1.0% in the U.S. compared to gains of 7.3% in Europe and +4.4% in the rest of the Americas (excluding the U.S.).

By brand, Hilton’s (HLT) Waldorf Astoria hotels realized the largest average daily rate, or ADR, (+2.2%) of the company’s other properties, driving up RevPar by 5.3%, also the highest among the remaining properties. Conversely, ADR for Hampton by Hilton increased by a more modest 1.0% leaving RevPar nearly unchanged.

Hilton (HLT) shares were down 4% in Wednesday’s premarket trading with the company’s lowered outlook for FY24 weighing on shares of Hyatt (H), Marriott (MAR), Choice Hotels (CHH), and Wyndham (WH).

data.title: Creative Medical Technology to raise $1.9M via direct offering
data.description:
• Creative Medical Technology (NASDAQ:CELZ) said it is looking to raise $1.9M through a direct offering of shares.
  • The company said it will issue f 418.6K shares at $4.42 apiece.
  • The firm said it will also issue warrants to purchase up to 837.1K shares, which will have an exercise price of $4.42 apiece.

data.title: Lumos inks take-private deal with Double Point Ventures
data.description:
• Lumos Pharma (NASDAQ:LUMO) announced Wednesday a merger agreement with Double Point Ventures, under which the Florida-based venture capital firm is set to acquire the biotech in a take-private deal.
  • Per the terms, Double Point Ventures is set to buy Lumos Pharma's (NASDAQ:LUMO) common stock for $4.25 per share in cash plus a Contingent Value Right per share linked to certain milestones.
  • The rare-disease-focused biotech said its officers, directors, and shareholders owning about 17.7% of its stock have already agreed to tender their shares in support of the transaction. The deal is expected to close in Q2 2025.
  • Lumos (LUMO) also announced that the FDA has agreed to the company's proposed trial deals for its Phase 3 clinical study for its lead candidate LUM-201 in Pediatric Growth Hormone Deficiency. The global trial is expected to begin in Q2 2025, involving about 80 study sites.

data.title: Challenge to Shell stake sale in Rosneft refinery rejected by German court
data.description:
A German court has dismissed a complaint by Russian oil firm Rosneft against Shell's (NYSE:SHEL) planned sale of its 37.5% stake in Germany's Schwedt refinery to Britain's Prax Group, Reuters reported Wednesday.

Rosneft argued that, under an agreement among Schwedt's ownership consortium, existing shareholders had a right of first refusal and that having Prax as a stakeholder in Schwedt could jeopardize the sale of its own stake in the future.

The court said Rosneft's view of the buyer’s suitability was irrelevant to its obligation to approve the sale.

Rosneft's lawyer in Germany said the firm would consider all possible legal options, including an appeal to the Federal Court of Justice.

The lawyer also said the Prax deal could fall apart for other reasons, adding there was no sign of the transaction closing even though the contractual deadlines for that to happen had expired.

data.title: Oracle in focus as RBC Capital starts with Sector Perform rating
data.description:
• Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) was in focus on Wednesday as RBC Capital Markets initiated coverage on the IT giant with a Sector Perform rating and $165 price target.
  • Shares were down 0.4% in premarket trading.
  • While the Safra Catz-led Oracle has maintained a leadership position in database and is making strides in its cloud transformation, there are concerns about competition and how long the company can benefit from generative artificial intelligence boom and growth in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, RBC analyst Rishi Jaluria said. However, the company is in a "strong" position in enterprise resource planning, thanks to its Fusion and NetSuite offerings, with Fusion seen as "one of the more positive drivers" for the company. Eventually, a large part of the company's on-premise installed base will migrate to Fusion SaaS, where Oracle has seen annual recurring revenue rise four times, Jaluria explained.
  • Over the next several years, Oracle should see double-digit earnings growth, given it's a "highly profitable" business with company and cloud gross margins above 70%, Jaluria posited.

data.title: NextEra Energy in charts: Reports strong earnings growth across businesses in Q3
Deutsche Bank's credit loss outlook for Q4 deteriorates as headwinds persist
data.description:
Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB) stock fell 1.8% in Wednesday premarket trading after Germany's largest bank increased its outlook for its full-year provision for credit losses.

At the same time, though, the company is seeking authorization from the European Central Bank for its next share buyback.

Q3 GAAP EPS of €0.81 rose from -€0.28 in the prior quarter and €0.56 a year ago.

Q3 pretax profit, excluding a partial release of litigation provisions related to its takeover of Postbank AG, rose 6% Y/Y to €1.8B. After tax, its profit, excluding the Postbank release, increased 8% Y/Y to €1.2B.

Its Q3 provision for loan losses increased to €494M from €476M in Q2 and €245M in last year's Q3, as stage 3 provisions, mainly from its Private Bank, rose.

Deutsche Bank (DB) now expects its full-year provision for credit losses to be ~€1.8B, higher than its previous guidance as headwinds persist in 2024. Transitional effects from the Postbank integration led to longer-than expected impacts for its internal collection and recovery processes, and the bank was impacted by two larger and fast-paced corporate events. Also, its full-year commercial real estate provision run rate ran at a cyclically higher level, "but has now substantially declined quarter on quarter," Chief Financial Officer James von Moltke said.

He does see improvement in 2025. "We continue to expect an amelioration will follow next year, as the transitory headwinds we called out will pass, leading to a reduction towards more normalized levels," he said.

In its revenue outlook for Q4, the company expects its Corporate Bank revenue to remain essentially flat Q/Q as growth in noninterest revenue compensates for lower deposit income. In its Investment Bank, management expects strong performance across its businesses in FIC vs. Q4 2023. Its Q4 pipeline in Origination & Advisory, which increased Y/Y, "suggests positive momentum" for the rest of 2024.

The Private bank's revenue is expected to increase Q/Q on higher net interest income. Asset Management revenue is expected to grow as the momentum of the past six months accelerates in Q4, with potential upside from performance fees.

data.title: UP Fintech prices public offering of 15M American Depositary Shares at $6.25 per ADS
data.description:
• Online brokerage firm for global investors, UP Fintech Holding (NASDAQ:TIGR), said its public offering of 15M American Depositary Shares is priced at $6.25 per ADS.
  • The underwritten public offering was announced yesterday. Each of the ADS will represent 15 class A ordinary shares.
  • The offering is expected to close on October 24.
  • The net proceeds of ~$90M are expected to be used for strengthening the company's capital base and furthering business development initiatives.
  • TIGR shares were trading -2.17% Wednesday pre-market at $6.31.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Thermo Fisher slips after Q3 topline miss; guidance unchanged
data.description:
Thermo Fisher Scientific (NYSE:TMO) traded lower in the premarket on Wednesday as the Waltham, Massachusetts-based healthcare giant reaffirmed its 2024 revenue outlook after falling short of Street forecasts with its Q3 revenue.

A leader in the life sciences industry, Thermo Fisher (NYSE:TMO) reported $10.6B in revenue for the quarter, mostly unchanged from last year and missing the consensus by $30M as its main revenue generator, Laboratory Products & Biopharma Services segment, disappointed.

While revenue from Laboratory Products & Biopharma Services reached $5.7B during the quarter, unchanged from the prior-year period, Thermo’s (TMO) Life Sciences Solutions Segment added $2.4B to the topline, indicating ~2% YoY contraction.

However, TMO’s Analytical Instruments Segment brought $1.8B, and the Specialty Diagnostics Segment added $1.1B, implying ~3% YoY and ~4% YoY growth, respectively.

While its adj. gross margin slipped to 41.8% from 42.0% in the prior-year quarter and its adj EPS fell ~7% YoY to $5.28, Thermo Fisher (TMO) managed to beat Street forecasts with its earnings.

The company tightened its full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $21.35-$22.07, compared to its previous guidance of $21.29-$22.07, and reiterated its 2024 revenue outlook of $42.4B—$43.3B.
